source: shared ownership fake-transport planning from Android and iOS file utility error tests

expected.sharedOwnershipRequirement: Shared ownership of file utility orchestration requires fake PFTP request and write-stream tests that cover malformed directory payloads, request-level transport errors, response-error status mapping, and write-stream failures after the PUT header is prepared.
observations: captured command and path for every attempted operation
captured PUT payload before write-stream failure mapping
public Android and iOS facade error shape after shared runtime mapping
expected.commonDecision: Preserve documented platform-specific policies only where required for public compatibility; otherwise normalize shared runtime errors to typed request, parse, response, and write-stream failures.

platformExpectations.android: BDBleApiImplTest and file-facade-runtime-policy.json pin Android public low-level file response-error object/name mapping, empty-read success, write-stream failure, and delete request failure behavior
platformExpectations.ios: PolarBleApiImplTests and file-facade-runtime-policy.json pin iOS public low-level file response-error code mapping, empty-read success, write-stream failure, and delete request failure behavior
platformExpectations.common: executable shared commonTest covers file command capture and typed runtime errors before facade mapping moves

execution.reason: Shared commonTest now asserts command capture, malformed directory payload handling, directory-missing status 103, request transport errors, response errors, and write-stream failures through the common fake transport; Android and iOS public facade tests pin low-level file error mapping and empty-read behavior before production file orchestration delegates to shared code.

notes: This vector complements executable file read/write/delete and directory-listing vectors with shared commonTest runtime checks plus Android and iOS public low-level file facade compatibility through file-facade-runtime-policy.json; directory traversal and additional file workflows still need their own facade compatibility before production file orchestration delegates to shared code.
